name: How to Troubleshoot Indexing Issues with Google Search Console
description: Dive into the world of Google Search Console and learn how to identify, tackle, and conquer indexing issues that can hinder your website's search visibility. Explore common errors, discover solutions, and optimize your site's performance for enhanced SEO results.
main_title: Mastering Indexing Issue Troubleshooting in Google Search Console
richtext:
What are Indexing Issues in Google Search Console?

For webmasters, SEO specialists, and website owners, Google Search Console is extremely crucial. You can learn important details about how Google's search engine perceives and uses your website from this. You may find out information about your site's indexing quality, which is important for your online presence. Indexing issues in Google Search Console are issues that could prevent Google from effectively crawling or indexing your website. These problems may significantly affect how simple it is for users to find your website in search results.

‍

  • What They Are: Indexing issues happen when Google's bots face difficulties cataloguing a website's pages due to restrictions or server errors.
  • Consequences: Such issues can decrease a website's visibility in Google's search results, leading to less traffic and conversions.
  • Common Errors: Errors can vary, including "Crawl Anomalies", "URLs blocked by robots.txt", "Server error (5xx)", "Redirect error", and "Soft 404s".
  • Identification: The "Coverage" report in Google Search Console allows for efficient detection and understanding of these indexing issues.
  • Resolution: Issues can be fixed by identifying and addressing the source, followed by using Google Search Console's "URL Inspection" tool to request re-indexing.

How to Monitor Rich Results Performance and Identify Issues with Google Search Console's Report:

‍

  1. Sign in to your Google Search Console account. If you don't have one, create an account and verify ownership of your website.

‍

  1. Once you're signed in, From the property dropdown, select the website for which you want to troubleshoot indexing issues.

‍

  1. On the left-hand side of the Search Console dashboard, click on "Pages" under the Indexing section. This report displays the indexing state of all URLs that Google has visited or tried to visit, on your property.

‍

  1. The Coverage report will show a graph and a table with "Error", "Valid with warnings", "Valid", and "Excluded" tabs. Click on the "Error" tab to view the URLs that Google couldn’t index.

‍

  1. Each error type is listed in a separate row. Clicking on each row will open a page providing further information about the error and a list of example URLs from your website where the error was found.

‍

  1. Once you understand the nature of the errors, work on the necessary fixes. This might involve modifying your website's robots.txt file, fixing server errors, correcting faulty redirects, updating noindex directives, or resolving other site-specific issues.

‍

  1. After you’ve made the necessary changes, you can ask Google to validate your fixes. Click on the error you've fixed, then click on "Validate Fix". This process can take a few days.

 

‍

  1. Google Search Console will keep you updated on the validation process. If the fix is successful, the error will be removed from your report. If not, Google will provide information on what's still wrong.

‍

That's it! Successfully troubleshooting and resolving indexing issues in Google Search Console is crucial for optimal SEO performance. Regular monitoring and swift resolution of these issues ensure your website's visibility and accessibility on Google, driving higher traffic and conversions. In the fast-paced SEO landscape, continuous learning and adaptation are key. 

‍
